body_txt: The logical flipside of Adaptational Comic Relief : a character with a known sense of humor (or at least a lighthearted side) is made more serious or just humourless in an adaptation. Can result in a character being The Stoic . May also come from an Adaptational Angst Upgrade . May overlap with Adaptational Villainy or Adaptational Jerkass if the character's nastier or more sinister portrayal extracts them from any comedic moments they once had, although the opposite can also apply if the character in question was a Comedic Sociopath or a Smug Snake . This trope is often applied to The Hero , especially in film adaptations of comic books. It is also sometimes applied to villains who are Laughably Evil in the original story. Compare and contrast OOC Is Serious Business , which usually applies to characters going out-of-character to reflect their current situations within the works themselves and is not mutually exclusive to adaptation changes. Opposite of Adaptational Comic Relief , where an originally serious character becomes a Plucky Comic Relief . Sub-Trope of Adaptation Personality Change .
